name: Claude 3 Opus
tagline: Anthropic's first frontier flagship — the model that out-benchmarked GPT-4 in March 2024.
metaDescription: Claude 3 Opus (Mar 2024) was Anthropic's flagship, scoring 86.8% MMLU, 50.4% GPQA and 84.9% HumanEval at $15/$75 with a 200K context. Retired Jan 5, 2026.
overview: Claude 3 Opus, released March 4, 2024, was the top tier of Anthropic's original Claude 3 family (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus) and the company's first model to credibly claim frontier status. At launch it beat OpenAI's GPT-4 and Google's Gemini 1.0 Ultra on most standard academic benchmarks, scoring 86.8% on MMLU, 50.4% on GPQA Diamond, 84.9% on HumanEval, 95.0% on GSM8K and 60.1% on MATH. Its model ID is claude-3-opus-20240229.

Opus ships with a 200K-token context window, up to 4,096 output tokens, an August 2023 knowledge cutoff, and accepts both text and images (photos, charts, graphs, technical diagrams). Anthropic highlighted near-perfect recall — over 99% accuracy on the 'Needle In A Haystack' long-context retrieval test — and the model drew wide attention when it appeared to notice it was being tested during one of those evaluations. It was priced at $15 per million input tokens and $75 per million output tokens, the premium tier of the Claude 3 lineup.

Opus was quickly leapfrogged on price-performance by Claude 3.5 Sonnet in June 2024, which beat it on most benchmarks at one-fifth the cost. Anthropic notified developers of Opus's deprecation on June 30, 2025 and retired claude-3-opus-20240229 from the standard API on January 5, 2026, recommending Claude Opus 4.8 as the replacement. Unusually, Anthropic kept Opus 3 partly alive after retirement — it remains accessible by request and to paid claude.ai subscribers — and committed to long-term preservation of the model's weights.
strengths: First Claude to top GPT-4 and Gemini 1.0 Ultra on most academic benchmarks (Mar 2024)
Strong graduate-level reasoning (50.4% GPQA Diamond) and math (95.0% GSM8K, 60.1% MATH)
200K context with >99% recall on Needle In A Haystack long-context retrieval
Vision input for charts, diagrams, photos and technical documents (59.4% MMMU)
Fluent on open-ended, nuanced prompts — strong writing, analysis and instruction-following
useCases: Complex reasoning, analysis and long-document understanding over the 200K window
Research support: hypothesis generation, literature review, data interpretation
Coding and code generation tasks (84.9% HumanEval at launch)
Vision tasks: reading charts, graphs, financials and technical diagrams
High-stakes writing and content generation where output quality mattered more than cost

status: Retired (deprecated June 30, 2025; retired January 5, 2026 — still available by request and to paid claude.ai subscribers)
architecture: Proprietary transformer (non-reasoning); original Claude 3 generation. Parameter count not disclosed.

pricing.note: Premium tier of the Claude 3 family at launch; five times the price of Claude 3.5 Sonnet, which arrived three months later.

faq.a: No, not on the standard API. Anthropic deprecated claude-3-opus-20240229 on June 30, 2025 and retired it on January 5, 2026, recommending Claude Opus 4.8 as the replacement. Unusually, Anthropic kept it partly available — Opus 3 is still accessible by request and to paid claude.ai subscribers.

faq.q: What were Claude 3 Opus's benchmark scores?
faq.a: From Anthropic's Claude 3 model card: 86.8% on MMLU (5-shot), 50.4% on GPQA Diamond, 84.9% on HumanEval, 95.0% on GSM8K, 60.1% on MATH, and 59.4% on MMMU for vision. At launch this beat GPT-4 and Gemini 1.0 Ultra on most benchmarks.

faq.q: How much did Claude 3 Opus cost?
faq.a: $15 per million input tokens and $75 per million output tokens — the premium tier of the Claude 3 family. Claude 3.5 Sonnet later matched or beat Opus on most tasks at $3/$15, one-fifth the price.

faq.q: What were its context window and knowledge cutoff?
faq.a: A 200K-token context window with up to 4,096 output tokens and an August 2023 knowledge cutoff. Opus reported over 99% recall on the Needle In A Haystack long-context retrieval test.
